title: SARIF AI emit profile — novel rule descriptor object (--rules)
description: Input contract for a reportingDescriptor an AI producer supplies to add-reporting-descriptor --rules (stored under run.tool.driver.rules[]). It overlays ai-reporting-descriptor.schema.json by $ref — inheriting the non-empty string id requirement — and tightens the id to the full NOVEL- escape-hatch grammar the --rules path enforces (AIRuleIdConvention: NOVEL- followed by a lowercase-alphanumeric kebab sub-id, single hyphens, no slash, no leading/trailing/consecutive hyphen). This is the same grammar a result's NOVEL- ruleId must satisfy, so a descriptor id authored here matches the ruleId that references it. Taxonomy-mapped rule descriptors (e.g. CWE-89) come from the finalize-time taxonomy enricher, not this verb.

id.description: Novel-finding descriptor id (SARIF §3.49.3). The non-empty string requirement is inherited from ai-reporting-descriptor; this overlay tightens it to the full NOVEL- grammar so the descriptor id is byte-identical to the result ruleId that references it: NOVEL- plus a lowercase-alphanumeric kebab sub-id (single hyphens, no slash, no trailing hyphen). 'NOVEL-prompt-injection' is accepted; bare 'NOVEL-', 'NOVEL-foo/bar', and 'NOVEL-Upper' are rejected.
